title: Money Just Sitting in the Bank
scenario: Your savings account is growing and earning almost nothing. A colleague suggests a monthly SIP into an index fund. Another says keep it all in the bank where it feels safe. What do you do?

choices.label: Start a ₹5,000/month index SIP
choices.lesson: Starting early lets compounding do the heavy lifting. Even a small SIP, left alone for years, can outgrow a much larger amount started later.

choices.label: Keep everything in the savings account
choices.lesson: Idle cash quietly loses value to inflation. Once your emergency fund is set, long-term money should be invested, not parked.
